title: [main.c:117-119] init_standalone_lora calls lichen_lora_l2_stop on late failure but not cleanup link state
description: In init_standalone_lora() lines 105-119, if lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() returns NULL after start(), the code calls lichen_lora_l2_stop() to clean up. However, this leaves any state initialized by lichen_lora_l2_start() (like the LoRa radio config) in an inconsistent state. The function returns -ENODATA but the module is now in 'stopped but initialized' state. A subsequent retry would call init() again which is idempotent, but the radio may be in an unexpected configuration. Consider calling lichen_lora_l2_deinit() for full cleanup.
close_reason: Fixed: init_standalone_lora now uses goto cleanup pattern with both lichen_lora_l2_stop() and lichen_lora_l2_deinit() for complete cleanup on late failures. Also fixed pre-existing bug where lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort() was called unconditionally in lora_l2.c but only defined when CONFIG_LICHEN_L2 is enabled.
